subject: [PATCH v10 07/37] mm: thread user_addr through page allocator for cache-friendly zeroing

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the churn approach does not prevent developers from passing user_addr = -1 to alloc_frozen(), which can lead to a page being exported to userspace without proper cache flush, and suggested using alloc_page() + vm_insert() as an example of this vulnerability.
reviews.raw_body:
I agree that misuse can cause trouble. But if we do the churn approach,
what prevents developer from doing alloc_frozen(..., user_addr = -1)
and using the returned page for userspace? It is possible the allocated
page can be exported to userspace later.

BTW, that cache flush thing is fragile even today, you probably can
do alloc_page() + vm_insert() to get a page without doing proper flush
and export it to userspace. There seems to be no mechanism to
prevent that.

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the increased complexity of having two APIs (alloc_frozen and alloc_frozen_user) carries maintenance burden, but did not indicate plans to simplify or restructure in this patch
sentiment_signals: did not commit to addressing the issue
reviews.raw_body:
Oh of course, I said that elsewhere.  It leaves us in a spot where we're
not technically worse than we were yesterday - except that the surface
of the buddy has increased (developers need to know about 2 APIs instead
of 1).  That carries maintenance burden (if something in alloc_frozen()
changes, something in alloc_frozen_user() may need to change).

There's a careful dance here.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Michael Tsirkin
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch may not work correctly on all architectures, specifically mentioning arm32 where missing the cache flush would result in a performance hit but still produce correct results
reviews.raw_body:
Probably arch dependent. On arm32, I think if you miss the flush, then
PG_dcache_clean will be clear and then you get a perf hit but
it's still correct. Didn't check others.

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch is part of a larger series that aims to improve memory management in the Linux kernel. Specifically, it uses the folio_zero_user function for user pages in the post_alloc_hook. The goal is to make the code more efficient and easier to understand by breaking down complex changes into smaller, more manageable pieces. The patch set has become unwieldy due to its complexity and scope, so Gregory suggests splitting it up into smaller pull-ahead sets that can be reviewed separately. This patch is one of the core improvements that will eventually lead to a final feature in multiple kernel versions.
